topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The music swelled, a vibrant, joyful symphony that seemed to vibrate right through my chest. I leaned against a marble pillar, a glass of champagne cool against my palm, watching the kaleidoscope of faces celebrating Sarah and Mark. A familiar shadow flickered near the entrance, and my breath hitched, a tiny, involuntary catch in my throat. It was him. Liam. He looked… different, polished, a successful architect with a confident smile. My gaze locked with his for a fraction of a second, and the warmth that had been settling in my core suddenly intensified, a pleasant, undeniable effervescence. He offered a brief nod, a gesture that felt both nostalgic and entirely forward-looking, before he moved on. I took a deep, steadying breath, the air tasting crisp and celebratory. I turned back to the dance floor, my smile widening naturally as I caught the eye of a close friend, feeling buoyant and utterly present in the moment.

[story 2]

I was practically glowing. The sheer energy of the reception was infectious; laughter erupted around me, and the clinking of glasses sounded like tiny bells. I was mid-conversation with Chloe, recounting a ridiculous anecdote from last week, when I spotted him across the room. Liam. He was standing near the buffet, laughing easily with a group of older acquaintances. My shoulders relaxed instantly, the tension that had been subtly coiled in my back dissolving like sugar in hot tea. A genuine, unguarded warmth spread through my limbs, making me want to move, to bridge the distance between us. When our eyes met, there was a shared, unspoken understanding—a quiet recognition that settled deep within my bones, a profound sense of ease. I didn't say anything, just offered a bright, open smile, and the feeling of contentment washed over me, making the rest of the evening seem perfectly calibrated.

[story 3]

The atmosphere was electric, thick with celebration and good cheer. I navigated through the crowd, feeling light on my feet, my steps quickening with the rhythm of the upbeat playlist. Then, I saw him near the bar, looking relaxed and utterly pleased with himself. My heart gave a little skip, a quick, energetic flutter that made my cheeks feel warm. It was Liam. He looked up, and for a fleeting moment, the noise of the party seemed to recede. A soft, knowing curve touched his lips, and in that instant, everything felt right. I stopped walking, letting the feeling settle—a deep, satisfying sense of peace mixed with a sparkling excitement. I felt a genuine urge to walk over, to share a moment, and the sheer buoyancy of the occasion made me feel completely capable of reaching out.
